_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn about reasons for competing claims on matters of fact and value, including the role of evidence and expertise.

_source.w: We learn about reasons for competing claims on matters of fact and value, including the role of evidence and expertise.
y: Understanding why claims compete helps us navigate disagreement more effectively.
Distinguishing matters of fact from matters of value clarifies what is really at stake in a debate.
Evaluating evidence and expertise helps us determine which claims deserve the most weight.
eg: We distinguish between factual claims (can be verified) and value claims (reflect priorities).
We explore why experts sometimes disagree and how to evaluate competing expert opinions.
We analyse the evidence behind competing claims and assess its quality and relevance.
We discuss how different values can lead to different conclusions even when the evidence is the same.
We evaluate claims by checking whether they are supported by strong or weak evidence.
We investigate how media can present competing claims in ways that are misleading.
